text: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally. [Entrepreneurial story](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/ondernemershalen/index.html). [Healthcare](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/index.html). 20/05/2025 • 6 minutes reading. [Nehreen Al-Mahmoedi](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/nehreen-al-mahmoedi/index.html). Is treating patients with complex psychiatric problems completely online impersonal? “Our patients think that there is very warm contact,” says Alex Roomer, operations manager of healthcare institution GGZ Online. A look behind the scenes shows that it is possible to have sufficient staff, manageable waiting times and a short treatment duration in specialized mental health care. Alex Roomer has more than 25 years of experience in Mental Health Care (GGZ) and currently works as a manager at GGZ Online. Roomer tells us about all the possibilities of fully digital care: “From the conversations between practitioner and patient that take place via video calling services and the coffee moments between colleagues, to tools such as virtual reality glasses that can help patients: literally everything goes digital.”. GGZ Online offers fully digital care to patients within specialized mental health care. At GGZ Online, a broad reflection of complex psychiatric problems is discussed. This varies from anxiety and obsessive-compulsive disorders and depression to complex traumas with PTSD complaints.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: GGZ Online operates as the digital outpatient clinic of GGZ Centraal and has experienced enormous growth since its founding in 2020. In 2020, the service started with 320 patients, in 2025 there will be 1200 and in 2026 GGZ Online expects to be able to treat 1800 patients.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally
GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: Relatively short, but highly intensive treatment processes in which the patient receives help from various professionals from a multidisciplinary team. Treatment at GGZ Online leads to reduced complaints.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: Over a period of up to three months, the patient receives intensive care several times a week via a video calling service. The patient and healthcare provider jointly make a decision about the treatment plan that the patient will follow.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: Professionals from multiple specialties then look at different facets of the complaints. For example, the basic treatment team includes a mental health psychologist, master psychologist and nurse. For example, a nurse thinks about lifestyle and behavioral changes. Where necessary, the patient receives additional help from, for example, a psychiatrist, cognitive behavioral worker or expert by experience.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: Roomer explains: “The treatment process is relatively short, but due to the intensity of the appointments and the versatility of the treatment plan, it is effective in reducing complaints.”. > “For us it takes approximately two to three weeks before the intake and three to four weeks before the treatment can start. We even want to reduce the total waiting time to two weeks.”.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text:
Alex Roomer   
Operations manager of healthcare institution GGZ Online. It appears that, despite the complexity of the complaints, 55 percent of GGZ Online patients make a full recovery, measured with the Outcome Questionnaire 45 (OQ-45). About 37 percent have less intensive complaints after treatment and can continue with less intensive care. Furthermore, patient satisfaction is higher than the national average, with an average score of 8.7.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: The main advantage lies in the short treatment time. Treatment at GGZ Online is four to five times faster than that of a comparable patient group in regular outpatient clinics with face-to-face contact. Roomer emphasizes: “For the patient, this means both health benefits and the opportunity to participate in society again more quickly.”. “With digital care, innovative tools and sufficient staff, we can keep waiting times and costs low,” says Roomer.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: "For us it takes approximately two to three weeks before the intake and three to four weeks before the treatment can start. We even want to reduce the total waiting time to two weeks." This is exceptionally short, given that the average waiting time for physical specialized mental health care is almost [21 weeks](https://www.rivm.nl/mentale-gezondheid/monitor/consequences-mentale-ongezondheid/wachttijden_specialistische_GGZ).

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text:
Digital care works well for patients and healthcare providers. Many patients find it a solution to do the sessions online. Especially with a high frequency of appointments, often several times a week, it is nice that a patient does not have to travel to a location and can rest at home immediately after treatment. The fact that GGZ Online operates completely digitally is also of great added value for regions with major staff shortages in mental health care. After all, GGZ Online is not location-specific and is therefore open to patients from all regions. > “It is possible for a colleague to go for a run in the morning and schedule a patient appointment in the evening.”. Alex Roomer   
Operations manager of healthcare institution GGZ Online. Of course, there is also a group for whom digital care is not suitable for certain reasons. For example, patients with an eating disorder or addiction require a longer care process than GGZ Online currently offers. There will also always be people who want and need face-to-face treatments. Roomer emphasizes: “It is not one or the other; it is actually good that the treatment is available both face-to-face and online.”
GGZ Online offers flexibility in working and living. GGZ Online's digital formula also manages to attract and retain staff in a tight labor market. Providing fully digital care offers employees flexibility in their schedules. The staff is free to schedule their own appointments and is not bound to the usual working day from 9 to 5. Roomer explains: “It is possible for a colleague to go for a run in the morning and schedule a patient appointment in the evening.”.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: In addition, about 30 percent of the staff works from abroad, for example as a digital nomad from a sunny country, their boat or camper. It is even possible to live abroad full-time and be employed at the same time. There are also practitioners who speak another language in addition to Dutch and English, which increases accessibility for patients with a different background. > “Our patients have pleasant experiences with our practitioners. The intensive contact gives the patient the feeling that they are heard and seen.”.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text:
Alex Roomer   
Operations manager of healthcare institution GGZ Online
Innovation breaks the conventional treatment pattern. The online conversations between the practitioner and the patient are the basis for the care that GGZ Online provides. In addition, the organization offers innovative tools and applications that contribute to recovery. This concerns, for example, virtual reality glasses for Exposure Therapy (VRET) to help people overcome anxiety disorders, or e-learning about how someone can improve their sleep hygiene and thus sleep quality. Or the sleep cuddle robot that gently breathes up and down and plays soothing sounds, helping the patient to relax and sleep. Roomer explains: "The combination of intensive contact, the available tools and the multidisciplinary team ensures that there is a holistic approach to treating the patient. This contributes to reducing complaints in a short time.".

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: Because GGZ Online does not have a physical location, the costs are relatively low.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: This means that no renovations or sustainability investments need to be made.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: In addition, GGZ Online uses AI tools for voice-controlled reporting, which can be used for intake reports and reports.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text: This means healthcare providers spend less time on administrative tasks, which means more time for patients. Don't be hesitant, but embrace digital care. With digital care, the feeling of personal contact remains alive. Roomer: "Our patients have great experiences with our practitioners. The intensive contact gives the patient the feeling that they are heard and seen." Roomer, who has decades of experience in mental health care, sees an important lesson in GGZ Online that can be valuable for other healthcare institutions: "Continue to discuss what is possible and share this with each other.

metadata.source_title: GGZ Online treats psychological problems completely digitally

text:
So much more is possible than people think."

The demand for care continues to increase due to an aging population, while it is hardly possible to attract more healthcare personnel. Innovation and collaboration make work easier and more fun, ensuring that healthcare provision remains sustainable and of high quality.
